stats.label: of photographers report having lost work to generative AI (Zenfolio 2026 survey, via PetaPixel, Jan 2026)
already had AI in their regular workflow as of the 2025 survey, mostly culling/masking/retouching, not full generation (Zenfolio 2025)
EU AI Act Article 50 transparency obligations take effect: generative outputs need machine-readable marking

synthesis: 2025 was the year AI imagery went from experiment to infrastructure, built directly into Google Ads, Amazon Seller Central, Uber Eats, Photoshop, and Shopify apps, while also the year public backlash got teeth, from Guess/Vogue to the Coca-Cola Christmas ad to Instagram's AI slop crisis.

The same pattern repeats across all five categories: low-budget, high-volume, catalog and menu and headshot work is being absorbed by AI first, while high-concept campaign, editorial, and identity work stays human-led but increasingly AI-assisted.

Disclosure is moving from voluntary ethics to legal and platform requirement, C2PA labeling, the EU AI Act, New York's Fashion Workers Act, so knowing how to disclose well is now a professional skill. Guess/Vogue is the cautionary tale; H&M's in-image "digital twin" label is the better-practice example.

The authenticity premium is the photographer's strongest counter-position: platforms and audiences are actively rewarding verifiably real, unpolished imagery precisely because AI polish has become cheap and everywhere.

platformPolicy.title: Meta: AI Info labels
platformPolicy.body: Instagram/Facebook/Threads label content as "Made with AI" using C2PA/IPTC metadata detection plus a manual self-disclosure toggle. Undisclosed AI content risks automatic labeling or reach penalties.

platformPolicy.title: TikTok: Content Credentials
platformPolicy.body: Integrated C2PA Content Credentials and requires user labeling of synthetic or manipulated content, run through multiple detection layers.

platformPolicy.title: Pinterest: user-facing AI filters
platformPolicy.body: Shipped per-pin AI controls and "see fewer AI pins" filters directly in response to AI slop backlash from users.

platformPolicy.title: EU AI Act Article 50
platformPolicy.body: From August 2, 2026, generative AI outputs distributed in the EU need machine-readable marking, and deepfakes need disclosure at first exposure. A Code of Practice on Transparency of AI-Generated Content is being finalized alongside it.

platformPolicy.title: New York Fashion Workers Act
platformPolicy.body: Effective June 19, 2025: clients must get clear written consent (scope, purpose, pay, duration) before creating or using a model's digital replica. The first US law aimed squarely at commercial-imagery likeness rights.
